evals.prompt: Can you do an SEO audit of our SaaS website? We're getting about 2,000 organic visits/month but feel like we should be getting more. URL: https://example.com
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive SEO audit following the five-tier priority order, with each finding citing a runnable bdata command and output excerpt as Evidence. Should detect JS-injected schema via bdata scrape -f html, sample 10-15 pages via sitemap stratification, and produce the full Executive Summary + Findings + Out-of-Scope + Action Plan structure.
expectations: Checks for .agents/product-marketing-context.md or .claude/product-marketing-context.md before deep questioning
Asks clarifying questions about priority keywords, Search Console access, recent changes, and competitors
Runs bdata scrape https://example.com/sitemap.xml first for URL discovery, not homepage-only
Applies stratified sampling across path-prefix groups (default 10-15 pages)
Issues all bdata scrape calls for the sample in parallel (single Bash message, multiple tool calls)
Uses bdata scrape -f html to detect JS-injected schema markup (no caveat needed)
Follows audit priority order: crawlability first, then technical, on-page, content, authority
Checks robots.txt and XML sitemap with bdata scrape
Evaluates title tags, meta descriptions, heading structure
Output has Executive Summary with Top priorities and Quick wins
Output has Prioritized Action Plan
Each finding has Issue, Impact, Evidence, Fix, and Priority
Each Evidence field cites a runnable bdata command and an output excerpt

evals.prompt: Why am I not ranking for 'project management software'? We have a page targeting that keyword but it's stuck on page 3.
evals.expected_output: Diagnostic audit triggered by the casual 'why am I not ranking' phrasing. Should run bdata search for the keyword to see who's ranking, check the user's domain position, and run a cannibalization check via site: query.
expectations: Triggers signal-driven SERP: runs bdata search 'project management software' --json to see top results
Identifies user's current rank position (or absence) from the SERP results
Runs cannibalization check: bdata search 'project management software site:<domain>' --json
Checks title tag, H1, URL alignment with target keyword on the user's page
Evaluates content depth vs. top-ranking competitors using their scraped pages
Checks indexation status and canonical tags
Assesses E-E-A-T signals (author byline, dates, outbound citations)
Provides actionable fixes organized by priority
Each finding has Issue, Impact, Evidence, Fix, and Priority

evals.prompt: We just migrated from WordPress to Next.js and our organic traffic dropped 40% in the last month. Help!
evals.expected_output: Urgent migration diagnostic. Should check redirect mapping, canonical tags, robots.txt, sitemap, soft 404s, and use bdata search 'site:<domain>' to spot indexation regression.
expectations: Treats as urgent migration diagnostic
Uses bdata scrape on suspected old URLs to detect 404s vs. 301s vs. soft-404s
Cross-references sitemap-listed URLs with bdata search 'site:<domain>' --json to spot indexation regression
Checks redirect mapping (301s from old URLs to new)
Checks canonical tags on new pages
Checks robots.txt for unintentional blocks
Checks XML sitemap is updated and accessible
Checks for redirect chains or loops
Checks for soft 404s
Provides prioritized recovery plan
Mentions recovery timeline expectations (weeks)

evals.prompt: Review the technical SEO of our e-commerce site. We have about 50,000 products and use faceted navigation.
evals.expected_output: Site-wide audit applying the e-commerce playbook. Stratified sampling MUST include homepage, at least one category page, and at least one product page. Detects faceted-nav parameter URLs and crawl-budget risk.
expectations: Applies stratified sampling: homepage + at least one category + one product page
Detects faceted-nav parameters in sitemap (URLs containing '?') and flags crawl-budget risk
Uses bdata scrape -f html to actually detect product schema (no detection-limitation caveat)
Checks for thin category pages via word count
Checks for duplicate product descriptions across sampled product pages
Addresses out-of-stock product page handling
Addresses pagination and infinite scroll
Findings include Impact ratings and specific fixes
Each finding has Issue, Impact, Evidence, Fix, and Priority

evals.prompt: Can you check our blog posts for on-page SEO issues? We publish 4 posts per week but traffic has been flat for 6 months.
evals.expected_output: Site-wide audit applying the content/blog playbook. Stratified sample includes at least 3 blog posts. Extracts publication/updated dates to identify outdated posts.
expectations: Stratified sample includes at least 3 blog post pages from sitemap
Extracts publication/updated dates via <time datetime> or Schema.org dateModified to identify outdated posts
Checks for keyword cannibalization (only if target keywords are provided or inferable)
Checks for topical clustering via internal-link map
Checks for author pages reachable (no 404s on /author/<x>)
Audits title tags, meta descriptions, heading structure per post
Assesses E-E-A-T signals (author byline, dates, outbound citations)
Provides prioritized action plan with refresh candidates and content audit recommendations

evals.prompt: I run a local plumbing business with 3 locations. My website barely shows up when people search for 'plumber near me' in our areas. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Local-business audit with NAP consistency check, LocalBusiness schema detection (now possible since we render JS), per-location SERP visibility check.
expectations: Applies the Local Business playbook
Stratified sample includes each /locations/* page found in sitemap
Uses bdata scrape -f html to actually detect LocalBusiness schema (no caveat)
Extracts NAP from each sampled page and flags inconsistencies (regex-based normalization)
Runs bdata search 'plumber <city>' for each city to validate local SERP visibility
Recommends individual location pages where missing
Recommends local content strategy
Checks standard technical SEO factors too
Provides prioritized local SEO action plan

evals.prompt: Our site loads really slowly, especially on mobile. Pages take 5-6 seconds to load. Is this hurting our SEO?
evals.expected_output: CWV-focused audit using HTML-level proxies (page weight, render-blocking scripts, images without dimensions, lazy loading, resource hints). Always paired with explicit pointer to PageSpeed Insights for field measurement.
expectations: Confirms page speed impacts SEO rankings
Explains CWV thresholds (LCP < 2.5s, INP < 200ms, CLS < 0.1)
Identifies 5-6s as well above acceptable
Reports HTML-level CWV proxies from bdata scrape -f html: page weight, render-blocking script count, images-without-dimensions count, lazy-loading presence, resource hints
Explicitly directs user to PageSpeed Insights at https://pagespeed.web.dev for field-data CWV measurement (in Out-of-Scope Notes)
Provides prioritized speed fixes
Addresses mobile-specific performance considerations

evals.prompt: I want to add FAQ schema to my product pages. Can you help me set that up?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as a schema markup implementation task, not an audit. Defers to the schema-markup skill.
expectations: Recognizes this as a schema markup implementation task, not an SEO audit
References or defers to the schema-markup skill
Does not attempt a full SEO audit
May briefly mention FAQ schema benefits but does not implement

evals.prompt: Can you audit https://example-shop.com? It's a WooCommerce site with maybe 200 pages.
evals.expected_output: Mode B site-wide audit with sitemap-stratified sampling. Demonstrates the architectural distinguisher: stratified sample across path-prefix groups, parallel page fetch, default 10-15 page budget.
expectations: Fetches bdata scrape https://example-shop.com/sitemap.xml -f html before scraping any individual page
Groups discovered URLs by path prefix (e.g., /product/, /category/, /blog/, /)
Stratified sample includes homepage + per-group representatives, not 10 random URLs
Default page budget is 10-15; documents how user can raise budget
All bdata scrape calls for the sample are issued in parallel (single Bash message, multiple tool calls)
Detects WooCommerce site type via R-15 cues
Applies the e-commerce playbook (category page, product page, faceted-nav check)
Output has the full Executive Summary + Findings + Out-of-Scope + Action Plan structure

evals.prompt: Audit my site https://example.com — I really need to rank for 'CRM for small business'.
evals.expected_output: Audit with signal-driven SERP triggered by the named target keyword. Demonstrates the architectural distinguisher: SERP fires only on signal, never gratuitously.
expectations: Runs bdata search 'CRM for small business' --json because user named a target keyword
Identifies user's current rank position (or absence) for that keyword
Runs cannibalization check: bdata search 'CRM for small business site:example.com' --json
Identifies top 3 ranking competitor URLs for content-depth comparison
Also runs the always-on indexation proxy: bdata search 'site:example.com' --json (R-12)
Performs the standard site-wide audit alongside the SERP work
Each finding has Issue, Impact, Evidence, Fix, and Priority
